figures.name: Alexander the Great
figures.significance: Conquered from Greece to India in 13 years; spread Hellenism across three continents

figures.name: Aristotle
figures.significance: Alexander tutor; his philosophy became the framework of Hellenistic, Islamic, and Christian scholarship

figures.name: Seleucus I Nicator
figures.significance: Founded dozens of Greek cities across Persia and Central Asia

figures.name: Tarquin the Proud
figures.significance: Last king; his tyranny led to the founding of the Republic

figures.name: Cincinnatus
figures.significance: Model of republican virtue; gave up power to return to farming

figures.name: Scipio Africanus
figures.significance: Defeated Hannibal; extended Roman power to Africa and Spain

figures.name: Cicero
figures.significance: Master orator; his speeches defined republican virtue; executed in civil war

policies.name: Pax Romana
policies.description: Period of relative peace and stability under the early emperors

policies.name: Edict of Milan — Christian Tolerance
policies.description: Constantine I granted tolerance to Christianity. Policy, not ideology — the empire remained officially pagan until Theodosius I.

figures.name: Augustus (Octavian)
figures.significance: Created the imperial system; the Pax Romana began with him

figures.name: Marcus Aurelius
figures.significance: Philosopher-emperor; his Meditations is the definitive Stoic text

figures.name: Constantine I
figures.significance: Legalized Christianity; founded Constantinople; shifted imperial ideology

name: Roman Empire (Christian)
note: Brief period when Christianity became the state ideology; unified empire before the East-West split

policies.name: Christianity as State Religion
policies.description: Theodosius I made Christianity the only legal religion. Hardened from policy into ideology.

figures.name: Theodosius I
figures.significance: Made Christianity the state religion; the last emperor to rule unified empire

figures.name: Flavius Aetius
figures.significance: Last great Roman general; defeated Attila at Catalaunian Fields

figures.name: Romulus Augustulus
figures.significance: Last Western Roman emperor; deposed by Odoacer in 476

name: Byzantine Empire
note: Eastern Orthodox Christianity is inseparable from Byzantine identity; emperor as head of both church and state

figures.name: Justinian I
figures.significance: Reconquered the Western Mediterranean; codified Roman law (Corpus Juris Civilis)

figures.name: Basil II (Bulgaroktonos)
figures.significance: Expanded Byzantium to its greatest medieval extent

figures.name: Constantine XI Palaiologos
figures.significance: Died defending Constantinople in 1453; the last Roman emperor
